page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 15 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, the hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations reveal strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1916.0 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 14.04 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show relative weaknesses. The Geekbench multi-core score is 4292.0, and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is measured at 3746.88. Uncached memory bandwidth for large blocks is limited, with read speeds at 29.4 GB/sec and write speeds at 18.6 GB/sec. In contrast, cached small-block memory read bandwidth performs at an average level of 335.06 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and strong single-threaded capabilities, the c4-standard-4 is suited for workloads that do not heavily parallelize or demand massive memory bandwidth. It is appropriate for static web serving, which achieves a throughput of 8.14 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations, which reach 5.84 million operations per second. It also supports light language model inference, processing 459.57 tokens per second for small 135M models. The lack of local storage and lower multi-core efficiency suggest it is best utilized as a cost-efficient node for single-threaded tasks, basic web hosting, and lightweight utility services rather than heavy database operations or parallel software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 260 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1916.0 and efficient single-threaded text processing. However, multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 4292.0 and a 18.6 GB/sec large-block write speed. This server is best suited for workloads prioritizing single-threaded execution, such as static web serving, Redis key-value operations, and lightweight utility tasks, rather than highly parallelized database or compilation workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, offering strong single-core performance for web serving and Redis workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for single-threaded and web serving workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server offering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for web serving and lightweight utility workloads.

Strong single-core performance with a 1916.0 Geekbench single-core score
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Average performance for static web serving and Redis operations
Suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight utility services, and basic web hosting
